title: agropyron cristatum subsp. michnoi
intro: Agropyron cristatum subsp. michnoi, also known as Michno's needle grass, is a subspecies of the crested wheatgrass plant native to the grasslands of the Canadian Prairies. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Agropyron cristatum subsp. michnoi include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used to support overall well-being and to promote a healthy immune system.
